markets.early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if the vote occurs.
markets.no_sub_title: Above 80
markets.rules_primary: If the number of Senate voting Yea on the next government funding bill before Jan 1, 2027 is above 80, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary:
A government funding bill is any legislation that appropriates federal spending in any amount.

The vote must occur in the relevant full chamber (that is, not a Committee vote, though not every member of the chamber needs to have participated in the vote). If no vote has occurred before the specified date, then any market not including "zero" within its count (e.g. "Between 50 and 64") resolves to No, and any market including "zero" (e.g. "Below 50") resolves to Yes. If the vote count is not specified but the bill is passed, any market including "100" for the Senate and "435" for the House of Representatives (e.g. "Between 65 and 100" or "Between 300 and 435") will resolve to Yes, and any market not including "100" or "435", respectively, (e.g. "Below 65") resolves to No.

Once the market has expired, a request by any Member of Congress to add, remove, or alter a vote shall not be considered for purposes of resolving this market, regardless of any change to the official vote tally reported by the website Congress.gov or any other official source.
markets.title: How many Senate members will vote Yea on the next government funding bill?
markets.yes_sub_title: Above 80

markets.no_sub_title: Whistleblower
markets.rules_primary: If any participating witness says Whistleblower as part of Senate Committee on the Judiciary: Arctic Frost: A Modern Watergate hearing, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary:
Video of the Senate Committee on the Judiciary: Arctic Frost: A Modern Watergate hearing will be primarily used to resolve the market; if a consensus by Kalshi employees cannot be reached using video, internal & external (official) transcripts of the Senate Committee on the Judiciary: Arctic Frost: A Modern Watergate hearing will be used.

Payout Criterion: 
The exact phrase/word, or a plural or possessive form of the phrase/word, must be used. Grammatical/tense inflections are otherwise not included. 
Plural nouns occurring within proper nouns and noun phrases (e.g. names, titles, etc) are included.
For the purpose of this market, the live broadcast or stream will be used to resolve this event. 
The event must be opened to the press for a live televised or streamed event to qualify.
Except as otherwise specified, previously aired content, archival footage, and recordings captured at times, places, or events wholly separate from the featured address will not count.
Please see full rules for more details.

Event does not qualify:
If an event is definitively cancelled or the event fails to qualify under the Payout Criterion, or any other circumstance prevents normal resolution, then the market for "Event does not qualify" resolves to Yes and all other markets will resolve to No.  
The event will resolve based on any participating witness speaking in an official capacity. If any participating witness is or is not present during a livestream, and does not speak in an official capacity the market will not qualify (e.g., inaudible private conversations on camera, unofficial statements picked up on a hot mic).

Postponement:
If the event is postponed, and such a postponement is announced by the end of the following calendar day to be rescheduled to a time within 14 calendar days, the markets shall remain open.

Video of the Senate Committee on the Judiciary: Arctic Frost: A Modern Watergate hearing will be primarily used to resolve the market; if a consensus by Kalshi employees cannot be reached using video, internal & external (official) transcripts of the Senate Committee on the Judiciary: Arctic Frost: A Modern Watergate hearing will be used.

Payout Criterion: 
The exact phrase/word, or a plural or possessive form of the phrase/word, must be used. Grammatical/tense inflections are otherwise not included. 
Plural nouns occurring within proper nouns and noun phrases (e.g. names, titles, etc) are included.
For the purpose of this market, the live broadcast or stream will be used to resolve this event. 
The event must be opened to the press for a live televised or streamed event to qualify.
Except as otherwise specified, previously aired content, archival footage, and recordings captured at times, places, or events wholly separate from the featured address will not count.
Please see full rules for more details.

Event does not qualify:
If an event is definitively cancelled or the event fails to qualify under the Payout Criterion, or any other circumstance prevents normal resolution, then the market for "Event does not qualify" resolves to Yes and all other markets will resolve to No.  
The event will resolve based on any participating witness speaking in an official capacity. If any participating witness is or is not present during a livestream, and does not speak in an official capacity the market will not qualify (e.g., inaudible private conversations on camera, unofficial statements picked up on a hot mic).

Postponement:
If the event is postponed, and such a postponement is announced by the end of the following calendar day to be rescheduled to a time within 14 calendar days, the markets shall remain open.

markets.no_sub_title: Miguel Damas
markets.rules_primary: If Miguel Damas wins the Altmaier vs Damas professional tennis match in the 2026 ATP Challenger Naples Round Of 32 after a ball has been played, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary: The following market refers to the Altmaier vs Damas professional tennis match in the 2026 ATP Challenger Naples Round Of 32 after a ball has been played.
